children.heading: Development of uniform methodology
children.label: 1 Development of uniform methodology
children.text: The Secretary shall direct the Commissioner of Education Statistics to convene a series of forums to develop nationally consistent methodologies for reporting costs incurred by postsecondary institutions in providing postsecondary education.

children.heading: Redesign of data systems
children.label: 2 Redesign of data systems
children.text: On the basis of the methodologies developed pursuant to paragraph (1), the Secretary shall redesign relevant parts of the postsecondary education data systems to improve the usefulness and timeliness of the data collected by such systems.

children.label: i
children.text: tuition and fees for a full-time undergraduate student;

children.label: ii
children.text: cost of attendance for a full-time undergraduate student, consistent with the provisions of section 1087 of this title;ll

children.label: I
children.text: each type of assistance or benefit described in ;section 1078(a)(2)(C)(ii) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: fellowships; and

children.label: III
children.text: institutional and other assistance; and

children.label: iv
children.text: number of students receiving financial assistance described in each of subclauses (I), (II), and (III) of clause (iii);

children.label: B
children.text: not later than 90 days after , report the definitions to each institution of higher education and within a reasonable period of time thereafter inform the authorizing committees of those definitions; andOctober 7, 1998

children.label: C
children.text: collect information regarding the data elements described in subparagraph (A) with respect to at least all institutions of higher education participating in programs under subchapter IV, beginning with the information from academic year 2000–2001 and annually thereafter.

children.heading: Data dissemination
children.label: b Data dissemination
children.text: The Secretary shall make available the data collected pursuant to subsection (a). Such data shall be available in a form that permits the review and comparison of the data submissions of individual institutions of higher education. Such data shall be presented in a form that is easily understandable and allows parents and students to make informed decisions based on the costs for typical full-time undergraduate students.

children.label: A
children.text: the change in tuition and fees compared with the consumer price index and other appropriate measures of inflation;

children.label: B
children.text: faculty salaries and benefits;

children.label: C
children.text: administrative salaries, benefits and expenses;

children.label: D
children.text: academic support services;

children.label: E
children.text: research;

children.label: F
children.text: operations and maintenance; and

children.label: G
children.text: institutional expenditures for construction and technology and the potential cost of replacing instructional buildings and equipment.

children.label: A
children.text: changes over time in the expenditures identified in paragraph (1);

children.label: B
children.text: the relationship of the expenditures identified in paragraph (1) to college costs; and

children.label: C
children.text: the extent to which increases in institutional financial aid and tuition discounting practices affect tuition increases, including the demographics of students receiving such discounts, the extent to which financial aid is provided to students with limited need in order to attract a student to a particular institution, and the extent to which Federal financial aid, including loan aid, has been used to offset the costs of such practices.

children.heading: Final report
children.label: 3 Final report
children.text: The Commissioner of Education Statistics shall submit a report regarding the findings of the study required by paragraph (1) to the appropriate committees of Congress not later than .September 30, 2002

children.heading: Higher education market basket
children.label: 4 Higher education market basket
children.text: The Bureau of Labor Statistics, in consultation with the Commissioner of Education Statistics, shall develop a higher education market basket that identifies the items that comprise the costs of higher education. The Bureau of Labor Statistics shall provide a report on the market basket to the Committee on Labor and Human Resources of the Senate and the Committee on Education and the Workforce of the House of Representatives not later than .September 30, 2002

children.heading: Fines
children.label: 5 Fines
children.text: In addition to actions authorized in , the Secretary may impose a fine in an amount not to exceed $25,000 on an institution of higher education for failing to provide the information described in paragraph (1) in a timely and accurate manner, or for failing to otherwise cooperate with the National Center for Education Statistics regarding efforts to obtain data on the cost of higher education under this section and pursuant to the program participation agreement entered into under .section 1094(c) of this titlesection 1094 of this title

children.heading: Promotion of the Department of Education Federal student financial aid website
children.label: d Promotion of the Department of Education Federal student financial aid website
children.text: The Secretary shall display a link to the Federal student financial aid website of the Department in a prominent place on the homepage of the Department’s website.

children.heading: Enhanced student financial aid information
children.label: e Enhanced student financial aid information

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: 1 Implementation
children.text: The Secretary shall continue to improve the usefulness and accessibility of the information provided by the Department on college planning and student financial aid.

children.heading: Dissemination
children.label: 2 Dissemination
children.text: The Secretary shall continue to make the availability of the information on the Federal student financial aid website of the Department widely known, through a major media campaign and other forms of communication.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: 3 Coordination
children.text: As a part of the efforts required under this subsection, the Secretary shall create one website accessible from the Department’s website that fulfills the requirements under subsections (b), (f), and (g).

children.heading: Improved availability and coordination of information concerning student financial aid programs for military members and veterans
children.label: f Improved availability and coordination of information concerning student financial aid programs for military members and veterans
A
children.text: contains information, in simple and understandable terms, about all Federal and State student financial assistance, readmission requirements under , and other student services, for which members of the Armed Forces (including members of the National Guard and Reserves), veterans, and the dependents of such members or veterans may be eligible; andsection 1091c of this title

children.label: B
children.text: is easily accessible through the website described in subsection (e)(3).

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: 2 Implementation
children.text: Not later than one year after , the Secretary shall make publicly available the Armed Forces information website described in paragraph (1).August 14, 2008

children.heading: Dissemination
children.label: 3 Dissemination
children.text: The Secretary, in coordination with the Secretary of Defense and the Secretary of Veterans Affairs, shall make the availability of the Armed Forces information website described in paragraph (1) widely known to members of the Armed Forces (including members of the National Guard and Reserves), veterans, the dependents of such members or veterans, States, institutions of higher education, and the general public.

children.label: A
children.text: administered, sponsored, or supported by the Department of Education, the Department of Defense, the Department of Veterans Affairs, or a State; and

children.label: B
children.text: available to members of the Armed Forces (including members of the National Guard and Reserves), veterans, or the dependents of such members or veterans.

children.heading: Promotion of availability of information concerning other student financial aid programs
children.label: g Promotion of availability of information concerning other student financial aid programs
A
children.text: distributed directly to the student or to the student’s account at an institution of higher education; and

children.label: B
children.text: operated, sponsored, or supported by a Federal department or agency other than the Department of Education.

children.heading: Availability of other student financial aid information
children.label: 2 Availability of other student financial aid information
A
children.text: not later than 90 days after the Secretary receives the information required under paragraph (3), the eligibility requirements, application procedures, financial terms and conditions, and other relevant information for each nondepartmental student financial assistance program are searchable and accessible through the Federal student financial aid website in a manner that is simple and understandable for students and the students’ families; and

children.label: B
children.text: the website displaying the information described in subparagraph (A) includes a link to the National Database on Financial Assistance for the Study of Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics pursuant to paragraph (4), and the information on military benefits under subsection (f), once such Database and information are available.

children.label: A
children.text: promptly respond to surveys or other requests from the Secretary for the information described in such paragraph; and

children.label: B
children.text: identify for the Secretary any nondepartmental student financial assistance program operated, sponsored, or supported by such Federal department or agency.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish and maintain, on the website described in subsection (e)(3), a National Database on Financial Assistance for the Study of Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (in this paragraph referred to as the “STEM Database”). The STEM Database shall consist of information on scholarships, fellowships, and other programs of Federal, State, local, and, to the maximum extent practicable, private financial assistance available for the study of science, technology, engineering, or mathematics at the postsecondary and postbaccalaureate levels.

children.heading: Separate information
children.label: i Separate information
children.text: The STEM Database shall provide separate information for each of the fields of science, technology, engineering, and mathematics, and for postsecondary and postbaccalaureate programs of financial assistance.

children.heading: Information on targeted assistance
children.label: ii Information on targeted assistance
children.text: The STEM Database shall provide specific information on any program of financial assistance that is targeted to individuals based on financial need, merit, or student characteristics.

children.label: I
children.text: standard contact information that an interested person may use to contact a sponsor of any program of financial assistance included in the STEM Database; and

children.label: II
children.text: if such sponsor maintains a public website, a link to the website.

children.label: aa
children.text: whether the financial assistance is need- or merit-based; and

children.label: bb
children.text: by relevant academic majors; and

children.label: aa
children.text: searches the STEM Database for all financial assistance opportunities for which an individual may be qualified to apply, based on the student characteristics provided by such individual; and

children.label: bb
children.text: provides information to an individual for only those opportunities for which such individual is qualified, based on the student characteristics provided by such individual.

children.label: I
children.text: a recommendation that students and families should carefully review all of the application requirements prior to applying for any aid or program of student financial assistance; and

children.label: II
children.text: a disclaimer that the non-Federal programs of student financial assistance presented in the STEM Database are not provided or endorsed by the Department or the Federal Government.

children.heading: Compilation of financial assistance information
children.label: C Compilation of financial assistance information
i
children.text: consult with public and private sources of scholarships, fellowships, and other programs of student financial assistance; and

children.label: ii
children.text: make easily available a process for such entities to provide regular and updated information about the scholarships, fellowships, or other programs of student financial assistance.

children.heading: Contract authorized
children.label: D Contract authorized
children.text: In carrying out the requirements of this paragraph, the Secretary is authorized to enter into a contract with a private entity with demonstrated expertise in creating and maintaining databases such as the one required under this paragraph, under which contract the entity shall furnish, and regularly update, all of the information required to be maintained on the STEM Database.

children.heading: Dissemination of information
children.label: 5 Dissemination of information
children.text: The Secretary shall take such actions, on an ongoing basis, as may be necessary to disseminate information under this subsection and to encourage the use of the information by interested parties, including sending notices to secondary schools and institutions of higher education.

children.heading: No user fees for Department financial aid websites
children.label: h No user fees for Department financial aid websites

children.text: a database or website of the Department that provides information about higher education programs or student financial assistance, including the College Navigator website (or successor website) and the websites and databases described in this section and ; orsection 1015a of this title
information about higher education programs or student financial assistance available through a database or website of the Department.
